qa_pairs.question: After performing recovery steps and unmounting filesystems, how can one restart the VM if they don't use the Ctrl+Alt+Del button on the OpenStack console?
qa_pairs.answer: After unmounting all real filesystems, you can stop and then start the VM from the OpenStack console page.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it safe to stop and start a VM after recovery operations when all real filesystems have been unmounted?
qa_pairs.answer: It is safe because all real filesystems are already synced, and all buffers have been flushed to the virtual disk, meaning operations were strictly in volatile memory.

qa_pairs.question: What user can be used to log in to the VM after it has successfully restarted following a Debian10 recovery procedure?
qa_pairs.answer: After the VM has started, you can log in as the root user with the password you chose during the recovery process.

qa_pairs.question: What security measures should be taken after successfully logging into a VM as root following a recovery operation?
qa_pairs.answer: Once completed, you should either remove the root password again or disable direct root logins via SSH.
